parameters.query:
-- Get the next pending clause that is NOT a header
-- Also get progress stats for the response
SELECT 
  c.clause_id,
  c.clause_number,
  c.clause_name,
  c.category,
  c.content as snippet,
  c.display_order,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM uploaded_contract_clauses WHERE contract_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'certified' AND COALESCE(is_header, false) = false) as certified_count,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM uploaded_contract_clauses WHERE contract_id = $1::uuid AND COALESCE(is_header, false) = false) as total_certifiable,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM uploaded_contract_clauses WHERE contract_id = $1::uuid AND status = 'failed' AND COALESCE(is_header, false) = false) as failed_count
FROM uploaded_contract_clauses c
WHERE c.contract_id = $1::uuid 
  AND c.status = 'pending' 
  AND COALESCE(c.is_header, false) = false
ORDER BY c.display_order ASC 
LIMIT 1;

UPDATE uploaded_contract_clauses 
SET status = 'processing', processing_started_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
WHERE clause_id = $1::uuid
RETURNING clause_id, clause_number, clause_name;

-- Get the full document text, contract context, and all clause numbers for boundary detection
SELECT 
  uc.extracted_text,
  uc.detected_contract_type,
  uc.contract_type_key,
  uc.initiator_party_role,
  (
    SELECT json_agg(json_build_object(
      'clause_number', c.clause_number, 
      'display_order', c.display_order,
      'snippet', LEFT(c.content, 80)
    ) ORDER BY c.display_order)
    FROM uploaded_contract_clauses c
    WHERE c.contract_id = $1::uuid
  ) as all_clauses
FROM uploaded_contracts uc
WHERE uc.contract_id = $1::uuid;

-- Update clause with certification results
UPDATE uploaded_contract_clauses SET 
  status = 'certified',
  content = CASE WHEN length($2) > length(COALESCE(content, '')) THEN $2 ELSE content END,
  original_text = CASE WHEN length($2) > length(COALESCE(original_text, '')) THEN $2 ELSE original_text END,
  clarence_certified = true,
  clarence_position = $3,
  clarence_fairness = $4,
  clarence_summary = $5,
  clarence_assessment = $6,
  clarence_flags = $7::jsonb,
  clarence_certified_at = NOW(),
  extracted_value = $8,
  extracted_unit = $9,
  value_type = $10,
  updated_at = NOW()
WHERE clause_id = $1::uuid
RETURNING clause_id, clause_number, status;

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contract_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.contractId, contract_type_key: $('Validate Input').first().json.contractTypeKey || null, initiator_party_role: $('Validate Input').first().json.initiatorPartyRole || null, roleContext: $('Validate Input').first().json.roleContext || null }) }}

parameters.query:
-- All clauses processed - mark contract as ready
WITH stats AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'certified') as certified,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'failed') as failed,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE COALESCE(is_header, false) = false) as total_certifiable
  FROM uploaded_contract_clauses
  WHERE contract_id = $1::uuid
)
UPDATE uploaded_contracts
SET 
  status = 'ready',
  clause_count = (SELECT total_certifiable FROM stats),
  parsing_notes = 'Certified: ' || (SELECT certified FROM stats) || '/' || (SELECT total_certifiable FROM stats) || 
    CASE WHEN (SELECT failed FROM stats) > 0 THEN ', Failed: ' || (SELECT failed FROM stats) ELSE '' END,
  processed_at = NOW(),
  updated_at = NOW()
WHERE contract_id = $1::uuid
RETURNING contract_id, status, clause_count, parsing_notes;

-- Mark clause as failed so chain continues to next
UPDATE uploaded_contract_clauses 
SET 
  status = 'failed', 
  clarence_assessment = $2,
  updated_at = NOW()
WHERE clause_id = $1::uuid
RETURNING clause_id, clause_number, status;

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contract_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.contractId, contract_type_key: $('Validate Input').first().json.contractTypeKey || null, initiator_party_role: $('Validate Input').first().json.initiatorPartyRole || null, roleContext: $('Validate Input').first().json.roleContext || null }) }}
